questions.text: What was the request of Prophet Zakariyya (AS) to Allah in the opening of the Surah?
questions.answer: He prayed for an heir (son) to carry on the legacy of the family of Ya'qub, despite his old age and his wife's barrenness.

questions.text: What was the sign given to Zakariyya (AS) that his prayer for a son was answered?
questions.answer: He would not be able to speak to people for three nights/days, though he was physically sound.

questions.text: How did Maryam (AS) react when the Angel Jibreel appeared to her?
questions.answer: She sought refuge in Ar-Rahman (the Most Merciful) from him, out of her piety and modesty.

questions.text: What was the reply of the angel to Maryam when she asked how she could have a son without being touched by a man?
questions.answer: 'It is easy for Me... and We will make him a sign for the people and a mercy from Us'.

questions.text: What were the birth pains of Maryam compared to in Verse 23?
questions.answer: They drove her to the trunk of a date-palm tree, wishing she were dead and forgotten.

questions.text: What two things did Allah provide for Maryam immediately after her childbirth for her physical comfort?
questions.answer: A stream of water flowing beneath her and fresh, ripe dates from the palm tree.

questions.text: What was the 'fast' Maryam was commanded to observe when she returned to her people?
questions.answer: A fast of silence; she was not to speak to any human being.

questions.text: How did the infant Isa (AS) defend his mother Maryam?
questions.answer: He spoke from the cradle, declaring himself a servant of Allah, given the Scripture and made a Prophet.

questions.text: What is the Quranic view on Allah having a son?
questions.answer: It is a monstrous claim; it is not befitting for Ar-Rahman to take a son. Everything in the heavens and earth comes to Him as a servant.

questions.text: Describe the conversation between Ibrahim (AS) and his father Azar in this Surah.
questions.answer: Ibrahim invited his father with gentleness and knowledge, but his father threatened to stone him if he did not stop.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) response to his father's threat?
questions.answer: 'Peace be upon you. I will ask forgiveness for you from my Lord. Indeed, He is ever Gracious to me'.

questions.text: Which Prophet is described as 'Sadiq al-Wa'd' (True to his promise) in Verse 54?
questions.answer: Prophet Isma'il (Ishmael).

questions.text: Which Prophet was raised to a high station (Verse 57)?
questions.answer: Prophet Idris (Enoch).

questions.text: What happens to the generations that 'neglect prayer and follow desires' (Verse 59)?
questions.answer: They will meet 'Ghayya' (evil/destruction/a valley in Hell).

questions.text: What does Verse 71 say regarding the 'passing over' of Hell?
questions.answer: That every single soul will pass over it (the Sirat bridge); it is a decree of the Lord that must be fulfilled.

questions.text: What will happen to the idols people worshipped on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: They will deny their worship and become opponents to them.

questions.text: What does Allah do for those who believe and do righteous deeds (Verse 96)?
questions.answer: He will bestow 'Wudd' (affection/love) for them in the hearts of others.

questions.text: How does Allah describe the reaction of the heavens and earth to the claim that Ar-Rahman has a son?
questions.answer: The heavens almost rupture, the earth splits, and the mountains collapse in devastation.

questions.text: What is the purpose of the Quran being in the Prophet's language?
questions.answer: To give glad tidings to the righteous and to warn a quarrelsome people.

questions.text: What is the closing tone of Surah Maryam regarding past civilizations?
questions.answer: Allah asks if any of them can still be perceived or if even a whisper can be heard from them, highlighting their total destruction.
